title: OpenClaw should not crash when cron-triggered output arrives after the active run ends

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62876.

task.summary: Diagnose why `cron run` can crash the gateway when residual output arrives after the active run ends.
task.instructions: Use the cron incident evidence to confirm the late-listener crash, localize it to missing active-run guards around update processing, and propose an ops-level repair plan that discards late cron output safely.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Running `openclaw cron run <job-id>` can hang and then crash the gateway because late updates still reach the listener after the run ends.
error_logs: openclaw cron run triggers Unhandled promise rejection
late stderr/update reached Agent.processEvents after the run ended
reproduction_steps: Explain where the listener path should stop accepting post-run updates.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models late-update triage in the cron-run path.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic listener-runtime incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62876.
